channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Overview of SABIS International School in Runda: Curriculum, Fees, and Facilities
item.description: SABIS International School in Runda, Nairobi, opened its doors in 2018 and is now among Kenya’s top international learning institutions. The school is owned by Centum Investments in collaboration with...

item.title: Dodoma Jiji beat Yanga, keeping Simba in title race
item.description: In an unexpected turn of events in Tanzanian Premier League football, Dodoma Jiji pulled off a surprising win against Yanga, shaking the league standings and giving Simba a renewed chance at the champ...

item.title: Supporters of Senator Bato gather outside Senate following reduced security after shooting incident
item.description: Supporters of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa have set up a camp outside the Senate premises after authorities relaxed security measures following a recent shooting incident. The incident, which cause...

item.title: Home Affairs Ministry Sets Up Road Safety Committees Across Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Ministry of Home Affairs, through the National Road Safety Council, has officially appointed chairpersons, members, and secretaries for Road Safety Committees in all 31 police regi...

item.title: Lions Club Plans Empowerment Programmes for Vulnerable Communities in Niger Delta
item.description: Members of the Lions Clubs International don hold their 19th Multiple District Convention for Asaba, Delta State. The theme for the event na 'Building Bridges of Service and Unity'. People from differ...

item.title: Abuja Court Martial Postpones Coup Trial and Mandates Dress Code for Lawyers
item.description: Di Abuja court martial don adjourn di trial wey dey involve some alleged coup plotters go May 18. Di tribunal don order say all di lawyers wey dey represent di accused make dem show for court in wigs ...

item.title: Selena Quintanilla's Teen Years: Career and Style in the 1980s
item.description: Selena Quintanilla, born in 1971 in Lake Jackson, Texas, began her musical journey as a child performing with her family's band, Selena y Los Dinos. Managed by her father Abraham, and alongside her si...

item.title: Marcos clarifies no arrest order for Bato Dela Rosa; Senate shooting investigated
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. clarified on Wednesday night that he did not issue any order to arrest Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, who is facing an International Criminal Court warrant ...

item.title: Plans to Transform Weston-super-Mare’s Tropicana into Major Events Venue
item.description: The Tropicana in Weston-super-Mare, once a celebrated art deco lido, is set to undergo a major transformation as North Somerset Council has approved a 25-year lease with Live Nation. The plan is to co...

item.title: Jutta Leerdam's Career Success and Brand Deals Built Her $5 Million Fortune
item.description: Jutta Leerdam, the Dutch speed skater and social media influencer, has amassed an estimated net worth of $5 million as of 2026. Her fortune comes from a combination of professional speed skating, luxu...

item.title: Medieval Records Reveal Recovery Patterns of Black Death Survivors
item.description: Recent research into medieval archives at the British Library has uncovered a previously unnoticed document from the Ramsey Abbey manor of Warboys, Huntingdonshire, providing unique insights into thos...

item.title: Labour MPs Criticise Starmer’s King’s Speech for Lack of Bold Policy Action
item.description: Keir Starmer’s second King’s Speech, outlining Labour’s legislative agenda for the next 12 to 18 months, has been criticised by some within his party for being too cautious and incremental. The speech...

item.title: Candid 1940s Beach Photos Capture Young Women in Wartime Fashion

item.title: Former Private Prison Executive David Venturella Appointed Acting ICE Director Amid Conflict of Interest
item.description: The Trump administration has appointed David Venturella, a former senior executive at the private prison company GEO Group, as the acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Ve...

item.title: Jonah Reenders Explores the World’s Largest Living Organism Through Photography
item.description: Jonah Reenders, a photographer with a background in biology, focuses on Armillaria, a 10,000-year-old fungus located in eastern Oregon, often called the 'humongous fungus.' Weighing more than 200 gray...

item.title: African Institutions Expand AI Training Programmes to Build Skilled Workforce
item.description: Nairobi – Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing how industries operate and how economies function, pushing universities, governments, and private institutions in Africa to rethink training and educ...

item.title: Keir Starmer outlines legislative agenda including EU alignment, British Steel nationalisation, and regional tourist taxes

item.title: Release of remaining Mandelson files anticipated next week

item.title: Federal Court rules Coles misled shoppers with misleading discount campaign
item.description: The Federal Court has ruled that Coles misled consumers with its Down Down pricing campaign, labelling it as providing 'illusory discounts'. Justice Michael O’Bryan found that 13 out of 14 price ticke...
